claim: Fz/PCP-dependent Pk localizes to the anterior membrane of notochord cells during zebrafish neuralation.

result.claim: Fz/PCP-dependent Pk localizes to the anterior membrane of notochord cells during zebrafish neuralation.
result.reasoning: The searches for similar propositions and chunks, using various formulations of the claim, did not yield any direct evidence regarding the Fz/PCP-dependent localization of Pk to the anterior membrane of notochord cells during zebrafish neuralation. While one paper (Paper ID: 4423559) discusses planar cell polarity signaling and neurulation in zebrafish, it does not specifically mention Pk localization in the notochord in the context of Fz/PCP. Other retrieved propositions and chunks are unrelated to the specific details of the claim, focusing on different protein kinases, cell types, or biological processes.

evidence.text: The DNA sensor STING is dependent in the type I interferon (IFN) signaling pathway.
evidence.source: Neutrophil extracellular traps enriched in oxidized mitochondrial DNA are interferogenic and contribute to lupus-like disease

evidence.text: NMDAR and CaMKII signalling, and postsynaptically synthesized BDNF are required for the activation of TrkB in the stimulated spine.

evidence.text: SNO-GAPDH transnitrosylates DNA-activated protein kinase (DNA-PK).

evidence.text: N-WASP bound all SH3 domains of Tks5/FISH.

evidence.text: Fork reversal in vivo requires interaction between ZRANB3 and polyubiquitinated PCNA.

evidence.text: Long-term potentiation (LTP) was enhanced in the human glial chimeric mice.
